greeting: Morning, Haniel. CT Pros ships today — let's get after it.
hype: Four go-live tasks for CT Pros plus two GS-ERP gates to clear. Real work, real clients, real progress — this is the kind of day you finish and feel it.
notice: Have CT Pros's real company info and QuickBooks credentials ready before 10:30.

schedule.title: Fill real CT Pros company contact info
Connect CT Pros's own QuickBooks (fresh OAuth)
Re-curate material catalog for a GC
Deploy CT Pros to its own host/domain
Design + installation time benchmarks per project type

tasks.title: P1: Audit CRM list 901407469657
tasks.notes: Run clickup_get_list + clickup_get_custom_fields + clickup_filter_tasks on first page. Determines whether the CRM list has the schema expected before writing the migration script.

prep.what: Read-only ClickUp API audit of list 901407469657 — confirm field schema and data shape before writing the migration script.
prep.understand: You're at Phase 15 (ClickUp Migration & Cutover) and this is the gate task: if the CRM list fields don't match what the ERP migration expects, you need to know now, not mid-script. The list ID is 901407469657. Three API calls, a notepad, and you're done.
approach: Call `clickup_get_list('901407469657')` — confirm the list name and which folder it lives in.
Call `clickup_get_custom_fields('901407469657')` — log every field name, type, and ID.
Call `clickup_filter_tasks(list_id='901407469657', page=0)` to sample a page of live task data.
Write a quick map: ClickUp field → ERP column, flagging any that need transforms or have no match.
resources: ClickUp API v2 docs: `GET /list/{listId}`, `/list/{listId}/field`, `/list/{listId}/task`
Your existing ClickUp integration code in the ERP repo
Phase 15 migration planning doc

tasks.title: P4: Migration — add projects.project_class (if adopted)
tasks.notes: Conditional on t96. varchar(4), pattern [SML][FC][1-6]. Carries ClickUp's classification as legacy reference data.

prep.what: Add a nullable `project_class` varchar(4) column to `projects` via a Laravel migration — carries ClickUp's classification as legacy reference data.
prep.understand: This is conditional: only run it if t96 (the adoption decision task) is marked done. The pattern `[SML][FC][1-6]` is Size × Frame/Custom × complexity tier. It's additive and nullable — zero migration risk.
approach: Confirm t96 is done in your task tracker before touching code.
Generate the migration: add `$table->string('project_class', 4)->nullable()` with comment `'[SML][FC][1-6] ClickUp legacy ref'`.
Run `php artisan migrate` locally and verify the column appears in the `projects` table.
If populating it from ClickUp import, add `project_class` to `$fillable` on the Project model.
resources: `database/migrations/` — follow existing naming convention
`app/Models/Project.php` — for `$fillable` update if needed
Phase 15 migration plan for t96 context

tasks.title: Design + installation time benchmarks per project type
tasks.notes: KB 9.1: Track next 10 projects for design time. Interview install crew leads for installation times. Required for capacity planning.

prep.what: Data-collection session: gather design-time and install-time benchmarks per project type from ClickUp history and a quick interview with Eric/crew leads.
prep.understand: This is KB 9.1 — no code, pure knowledge capture. The data feeds capacity planning and the Production module. You need design time per project type (from last 10 completed projects) and install time per type (from Eric/crew leads). End of day is perfect: light, conversational, high-value.
approach: Pull the last 10 completed projects from ClickUp or the ERP — note project type and estimated design hours for each.
Ping Eric (15-min chat or Slack): 'How long does a standard kitchen install take? Bathroom? Custom cabinet run?'
Structure the results as a table: `project_type | avg_design_hours | avg_install_hours | variance_notes`.
Drop the table into the KB 9.1 doc or the app's time-standards module as the source of truth.
resources: ClickUp project history — filter by `status=complete`, recent date range
Eric's contact / Slack for install-time estimates
KB 9.1 doc in the project knowledge base
`TimeStandard` model or equivalent in the ERP codebase

tasks.title: Fill real CT Pros company contact info
tasks.notes: config/company.php + .env still hold placeholders inherited from the HighTech clone. Set real CT Pros name/address/phone/email before go-live.

prep.what: Replace every HighTech-inherited placeholder in `config/company.php` and `.env` with CT Pros's real name, address, phone, and email.
prep.understand: Go-live step 1. Every PDF, invoice, and outgoing email currently shows HighTech's identity — a hard blocker before CT Pros shows this to anyone. The hour includes a grep sweep for stragglers and a PDF smoke test.
approach: Open `config/company.php` — replace name, address, phone, email, logo path with CT Pros's real values.
Open `.env` — update `APP_NAME`, `MAIL_FROM_NAME`, `MAIL_FROM_ADDRESS`, and any CT Pros-specific vars.
Run `grep -r "HighTech" config/ resources/ database/` and kill any remaining hardcoded references.
Generate a sample invoice PDF in the app and visually confirm it shows CT Pros branding, not HighTech.
resources: CT Pros's real company details — confirm with Rafa if unsure of exact address or phone
`config/company.php`
`.env` and `.env.example`
Invoice/PDF generation route for the smoke test

tasks.title: Connect CT Pros's own QuickBooks (fresh OAuth)
tasks.notes: Inherited tokens were HighTech's and were never carried over. Run a fresh OAuth connect against CT Pros's own QBO company.

prep.what: Clear the inherited HighTech QB tokens and run a fresh OAuth flow to connect CT Pros's own QuickBooks Online company.
prep.understand: The app has no valid QB tokens for CT Pros — either nothing or HighTech's expired creds. Every QB sync silently fails until this is done. Have CT Pros's QBO admin login ready before you start; the OAuth flow itself is two minutes but troubleshooting without creds wastes the whole block.
approach: Find and clear old tokens: check `quickbooks_tokens` DB table or `storage/framework/` for any OAuth token file — nullify or delete CT Pros's row.
Navigate to the QB connect route (e.g. `/quickbooks/connect`) in the app and start the OAuth flow.
Log in with CT Pros's QBO admin credentials and authorize the app — confirm the redirect back succeeds.
Verify: run a test QB call from the app (list customers or chart of accounts) and confirm you get CT Pros data, not HighTech's.
resources: CT Pros's QuickBooks Online admin credentials (from Rafa)
`config/quickbooks.php` — client ID, secret, redirect URI
QB connect route in `routes/web.php` or `routes/api.php`
QuickBooks OAuth 2.0 playground at developer.intuit.com if you need to debug a redirect

tasks.title: Re-curate material catalog for a GC
tasks.notes: The 148-SKU HighTech tile catalog still seeds — wrong domain. Replace with a GC-appropriate catalog before go-live.

prep.what: Swap the HighTech tile catalog seed for a general-contractor catalog covering CT Pros's actual trades: framing, drywall, paint, flooring, roofing, concrete, fasteners.
prep.understand: CT Pros is a GC — the current seeded catalog is wrong-domain (tile shop). Any estimate Rafa builds will show tile SKUs. If he has a real pricebook, use it as the source; otherwise seed sensible GC defaults grouped by trade. The seeder is already wired — you're just replacing its data.
approach: Open `database/seeders/MaterialSeeder.php` (or equivalent) and locate the HighTech tile SKU block.
Ask Rafa: 'Do you have a materials list or pricebook you use day-to-day?' If yes, import from CSV; if no, proceed with GC defaults.
Replace with GC trade categories (Framing, Drywall, Paint, Flooring, Roofing, Concrete, Fasteners) — 10–20 SKUs per category.
Run `php artisan db:seed --class=MaterialSeeder`, open the catalog in the app UI, and verify the trade categories appear correctly.
resources: Rafa's materials list or pricebook if available
`database/seeders/MaterialSeeder.php` (or equivalent seeder file)
`app/Models/Material.php` or `MaterialProduct.php` for the schema
CT Pros's typical project types to prioritize trade categories

tasks.title: Deploy CT Pros to its own host/domain
tasks.notes: Deploy infra exists and is proven (deploy/*.sh, docker-compose.yml, docs/deployment.md) but still pointed at the clone server. Update and run against CT Pros's own host.

prep.what: Re-point the proven deploy scripts at CT Pros's own server and domain, then run a clean deploy.
prep.understand: The hard part is done — `deploy/*.sh`, `docker-compose.yml`, and `docs/deployment.md` are battle-tested from the HighTech clone. You're not building new infra, just changing targets. Main risks: env var mismatches and DNS propagation lag. Read the deploy doc first so there are no surprises.
approach: Read `docs/deployment.md` fully — note any manual pre-steps or post-deploy checks.
Update `deploy/*.sh` env vars: server IP/SSH host, domain, any CT Pros-specific secrets.
Update `.env.production` (or equivalent): `APP_URL` to CT Pros's domain, `DB_*` credentials, `APP_KEY` if new.
Run the deploy script, watch the output for errors, then hit the CT Pros domain in a browser and confirm the app loads with CT Pros branding.
resources: `docs/deployment.md` — the authoritative deploy guide
`deploy/` directory — all deploy scripts
CT Pros server SSH credentials and domain/DNS access (from Rafa or your infra notes)
DNS propagation checker if the domain takes time to resolve
